Although perfect normality is not required for forecasting purposes, the absence of strong skewness or heavy tails supports the statistical validity of the model.

Overall, these diagnostic results indicate that the ARIMAX v2 specification sufficiently captures the temporal structure of the influenza series and produces residuals that behave like white noise. This provides further evidence that the model is appropriate for forecasting influenza activity.

# 7. ARIMAX V2 Monthly Robustness Check

```{r}
############################################################
# Monthly ARIMAX v2 robustness check
# (same naming style as the current Rmd)
############################################################

library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(forecast)
library(ggplot2)

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Build monthly covariates from the WEEKLY full covariate objects
#    already used in the weekly ARIMAX section
# ----------------------------------------------------------
df_monthly_xreg <- data.frame(
  month_start = floor_date(df_eda$WEEK_START, "month"),
  covid   = as.numeric(covid_dummy_full),
  ilinet  = as.numeric(ilinet_full),
  essence = as.numeric(essence_full),
  icu     = as.numeric(icu_full),
  peds    = as.numeric(peds_full)
) %>%
  group_by(month_start) %>%
  summarise(
    covid   = round(mean(covid,   na.rm = TRUE)),   # step dummy -> monthly dummy
    ilinet  = mean(ilinet,  na.rm = TRUE),
    essence = mean(essence, na.rm = TRUE),
    icu     = mean(icu,     na.rm = TRUE),
    peds    = mean(peds,    na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(month_start)

# Safety check: monthly covariates should align with df_monthly
stopifnot(nrow(df_monthly_xreg) == nrow(df_monthly))
stopifnot(all(df_monthly_xreg$month_start == df_monthly$month_start))

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Fill missing values (same spirit as your weekly fill_x)
# ----------------------------------------------------------
fill_x_m <- function(x) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  x <- forecast::na.interp(x)
  x[is.na(x)] <- 0
  return(x)
}

df_monthly_xreg <- df_monthly_xreg %>%
  mutate(
    covid   = fill_x_m(covid),
    ilinet  = fill_x_m(ilinet),
    essence = fill_x_m(essence),
    icu     = fill_x_m(icu),
    peds    = fill_x_m(peds)
  )

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Convert to monthly ts objects
# ----------------------------------------------------------
covid_m_ts   <- ts(df_monthly_xreg$covid,   frequency = 12)
ilinet_m_ts  <- ts(df_monthly_xreg$ilinet,  frequency = 12)
essence_m_ts <- ts(df_monthly_xreg$essence, frequency = 12)
icu_m_ts     <- ts(df_monthly_xreg$icu,     frequency = 12)
peds_m_ts    <- ts(df_monthly_xreg$peds,    frequency = 12)

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Use the SAME monthly split points as y_month_model
# ----------------------------------------------------------
covid_m_train   <- window(covid_m_ts,   end   = time(y_month_model)[n_m - h_m])
covid_m_test    <- window(covid_m_ts,   start = time(y_month_model)[n_m - h_m + 1])

ilinet_m_train  <- window(ilinet_m_ts,  end   = time(y_month_model)[n_m - h_m])
ilinet_m_test   <- window(ilinet_m_ts,  start = time(y_month_model)[n_m - h_m + 1])

essence_m_train <- window(essence_m_ts, end   = time(y_month_model)[n_m - h_m])
essence_m_test  <- window(essence_m_ts, start = time(y_month_model)[n_m - h_m + 1])

icu_m_train     <- window(icu_m_ts,     end   = time(y_month_model)[n_m - h_m])
icu_m_test      <- window(icu_m_ts,     start = time(y_month_model)[n_m - h_m + 1])

peds_m_train    <- window(peds_m_ts,    end   = time(y_month_model)[n_m - h_m])
peds_m_test     <- window(peds_m_ts,    start = time(y_month_model)[n_m - h_m + 1])

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Define monthly xreg matrices
#    baseline = COVID only
#    v2       = COVID + ILINet + ESSENCE + ICU + Peds
# ----------------------------------------------------------
xreg_m_base_train <- cbind(
  covid = as.numeric(covid_m_train)
)

xreg_m_base_test <- cbind(
  covid = as.numeric(covid_m_test)
)

xreg_m_v2_train <- cbind(
  covid   = as.numeric(covid_m_train),
  ilinet  = as.numeric(ilinet_m_train),
  essence = as.numeric(essence_m_train),
  icu     = as.numeric(icu_m_train),
  peds    = as.numeric(peds_m_train)
)

xreg_m_v2_test <- cbind(
  covid   = as.numeric(covid_m_test),
  ilinet  = as.numeric(ilinet_m_test),
  essence = as.numeric(essence_m_test),
  icu     = as.numeric(icu_m_test),
  peds    = as.numeric(peds_m_test)
)


# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 7) Fit monthly ARIMAX v2
#    Same monthly SARIMA skeleton + richer xreg
# ----------------------------------------------------------
fit_month_arimax_v2 <- Arima(
  train_m,
  order = c(1, 0, 0),
  seasonal = list(order = c(0, 1, 1), period = 12),
  include.constant = TRUE,
  xreg = xreg_m_v2_train,
  method = "CSS-ML",
  lambda = lambda_m,
  biasadj = TRUE
)

fc_month_arimax_v2 <- forecast(
  fit_month_arimax_v2,
  h = h_m,
  xreg = xreg_m_v2_test
)

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 8) Accuracy comparison
# ----------------------------------------------------------
acc_month_arimax_v2 <- accuracy(fc_month_arimax_v2, test_m)

get_test_row <- function(acc_mat) {
  as.data.frame(acc_mat)[2, , drop = FALSE]
}

monthly_arimax_cmp <- rbind(
  data.frame(Model = "Monthly SARIMA + COVID", get_test_row(auto_m_acc)),
  data.frame(Model = "Monthly ARIMAX v2",      get_test_row(acc_month_arimax_v2))
)

monthly_arimax_cmp_out <- monthly_arimax_cmp[, c("Model", "RMSE", "MAE", "MAPE", "MASE")]
print(monthly_arimax_cmp_out)

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 9) AIC / BIC comparison
# ----------------------------------------------------------
monthly_ic_cmp <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Monthly SARIMA + COVID", "Monthly ARIMAX v2"),
  AIC   = c(AIC(auto_m), AIC(fit_month_arimax_v2)),
  BIC   = c(BIC(auto_m), BIC(fit_month_arimax_v2))
)

print(monthly_ic_cmp)

# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 10) Observed vs Forecast plot
# ----------------------------------------------------------
actual_month_df <- data.frame(
  Time   = time(test_m),
  Actual = as.numeric(test_m)
)

base_month_df <- data.frame(
  Time     = time(auto_m_fc$mean),
  Forecast = as.numeric(auto_m_fc$mean),
  Model    = "Monthly SARIMA + COVID"
)

arimax_v2_month_df <- data.frame(
  Time     = time(fc_month_arimax_v2$mean),
  Forecast = as.numeric(fc_month_arimax_v2$mean),
  Model    = "Monthly ARIMAX v2"
)

forecast_month_df <- bind_rows(base_month_df, arimax_v2_month_df)


# ----------------------------------------------------------
# 11) Residual diagnostics for monthly ARIMAX v2
# ----------------------------------------------------------
summary(fit_month_arimax_v2)
checkresiduals(fit_month_arimax_v2)
```

To further assess the robustness of our results, we re-estimated the model using a monthly aggregation of the influenza series. Temporal aggregation provides a useful robustness check because it reduces short-term weekly noise and allows us to examine whether the key modeling conclusions remain stable at a lower frequency.

The monthly ARIMAX v2 model achieves lower RMSE and MAE than the monthly SARIMA model with only the COVID intervention term. In particular, RMSE decreases from 2.63 to 2.08 and MAE decreases from 1.79 to 1.57, indicating improved forecast accuracy when additional surveillance covariates are incorporated. Although the MAPE value is larger for ARIMAX v2, this metric can become unstable when the actual influenza positivity rate is very small, which frequently occurs during summer months. Therefore RMSE and MAE provide more reliable measures of model performance in this setting.

Information criteria also favor the richer specification. The monthly ARIMAX v2 model produces substantially lower AIC and BIC values (AIC = 283.4 vs 301.2; BIC = 305.3 vs 310.9), suggesting that the improvement in fit justifies the additional covariates included in the model.

Residual diagnostics further support model adequacy. The residual series fluctuates around zero without clear systematic patterns, and the residual ACF shows that most autocorrelations lie within the confidence bounds. In addition, the residual histogram appears approximately symmetric and centered near zero, indicating that the remaining errors are reasonably well behaved.

Overall, the monthly robustness analysis supports the main findings of the weekly analysis. Even after temporal aggregation, incorporating additional surveillance indicators such as ILINet, ESSENCE, ICU admissions, and pediatric deaths improves forecasting performance. This suggests that the predictive value of these covariates is not limited to short-term weekly dynamics but also persists at the monthly level, reinforcing the robustness of the ARIMAX-based modeling approach.

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)

# =========================
# 1. Monthly ARIMAX v2 forecast mean
# =========================
arimax_v2_mean <- as.numeric(fc_month_arimax_v2$mean)

# =========================
# 2. Rebuild actual monthly series
# =========================
df_month <- df_eda %>%
  mutate(month = floor_date(WEEK_START, "month")) %>%
  group_by(month) %>%
  summarise(
    flu = mean(LAB_FLU_PCT_POSITIVE, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Actual test values
h <- h_m
actual <- df_month$flu[(nrow(df_month) - h + 1):nrow(df_month)]
test_dates <- df_month$month[(nrow(df_month) - h + 1):nrow(df_month)]

# =========================
# 3. Build plotting dataframe
# =========================
plot_df <- data.frame(
  Date          = test_dates,
  Actual        = actual,
  SARIMA        = sarima_mean,
  ETS           = ets_mean,
  Naive         = naive_mean,
  SeasonalNaive = snaive_mean,
  ARIMAXv2      = arimax_v2_mean
)

plot_df_long <- plot_df %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Date,
    names_to = "Series",
    values_to = "Value"
  )

# =========================
# 4. Plot
# =========================
ggplot(plot_df_long, aes(x = Date, y = Value, color = Series)) +
  geom_line(aes(linewidth = Series)) +
  scale_linewidth_manual(
    values = c(
      "Actual" = 1.3,
      "ETS" = 1.0,
      "Naive" = 1.0,
      "SARIMA" = 1.0,
      "SeasonalNaive" = 1.0,
      "ARIMAXv2" = 1.0
    ),
    guide = "none"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Actual" = "black",
      "ETS" = "#d95f02",
      "Naive" = "#377eb8",
      "SARIMA" = "#32CD32",
      "SeasonalNaive" = "#7570b3",
      "ARIMAXv2" = "#e7298a"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Monthly Influenza Forecast Comparison",
    x = "Month",
    y = "% Influenza Positive",
    color = "Series"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 22, face = "plain"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 15)
  )
```

```{r}
get_test_metrics <- function(acc){
  acc["Test set", c("RMSE","MAE","MAPE","MASE")]
}

error_table <- rbind(
  SARIMA        = get_test_metrics(auto_m_acc),
  ETS           = get_test_metrics(ets_m_acc),
  Naive         = get_test_metrics(naive_m_acc),
  SeasonalNaive = get_test_metrics(snaive_m_acc),
  ARIMAX_v2     = get_test_metrics(acc_month_arimax_v2)
)

print(error_table)
```

# 6. CV for Best Model: ARIMAX v2

```{r, warning=FALSE}
y_full_ts  <- ts(y_full_pos, frequency = 52)


cv_expanding_arimax_v2 <- function(y, xreg,
                                   m,
                                   order,
                                   seas_order,
                                   horizons = c(4,8,12),
                                   initial = NULL,
                                   include.constant = TRUE,
                                   method = "CSS-ML",
                                   lambda = NULL,
                                   biasadj = TRUE) {

  hmax <- max(horizons)
  n <- length(y)
  if (is.null(initial)) initial <- floor(0.6 * n)

  origins <- initial:(n - hmax)
  n_origins <- length(origins)
  if (n_origins <= 0) stop("Not enough data for chosen initial window and horizon.")

  # safe xreg slicing (vector or matrix/data.frame/ts)
  x_slice <- function(X, idx) {
    if (is.null(dim(X))) return(X[idx])
    X[idx, , drop = FALSE]
  }

  out <- vector("list", n_origins * length(horizons))
  pos <- 0L

  pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = n_origins, style = 3)

  for (k in seq_along(origins)) {
    i <- origins[k]

    fit <- tryCatch(
      Arima(
        y[1:i],
        order = order,
        seasonal = list(order = seas_order, period = m),
        include.constant = include.constant,
        xreg = x_slice(xreg, 1:i),
        method = method,
        lambda = lambda,
        biasadj = biasadj
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )

    if (is.null(fit)) { setTxtProgressBar(pb, k); next }

    fc <- tryCatch(
      forecast(fit, h = hmax, xreg = x_slice(xreg, (i+1):(i+hmax))),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )

    if (is.null(fc)) { setTxtProgressBar(pb, k); next }

    for (h in horizons) {
      pos <- pos + 1L
      err <- as.numeric(y[i + h] - fc$mean[h])
      out[[pos]] <- data.frame(
        Method = "Expanding",
        OriginIndex = i,
        Horizon = h,
        Error = err,
        SqError = err^2
      )
    }

    setTxtProgressBar(pb, k)
  }
  close(pb)

  dplyr::bind_rows(out[1:pos])
}

# =========================
# Step 2b) Run expanding CV
# =========================
cv_exp_v2 <- cv_expanding_arimax_v2(
  y = y_full_ts,
  xreg = xreg_v2_train,
  m = m,
  order = chosen_spec$order,
  seas_order = chosen_spec$seasonal,
  horizons = c(4, 8, 12),
  initial = floor(0.6 * length(y_full_ts)),
  include.constant = TRUE,
  method = "CSS",
  lambda = lambda_bc,
  biasadj = TRUE
)

# Quick check: RMSE by horizon (expanding only)
cv_exp_v2 %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Horizon) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(RMSE = sqrt(mean(SqError, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop") %>%
  print()
```

```{r, warning=FALSE}
cv_sliding_arimax_v2 <- function(y, xreg,
                                 m,
                                 order,
                                 seas_order,
                                 horizons = c(4,8,12),
                                 window = NULL,
                                 include.constant = TRUE,
                                 method = "CSS-ML",
                                 lambda = NULL,
                                 biasadj = TRUE) {

  hmax <- max(horizons)
  n <- length(y)
  if (is.null(window)) window <- floor(0.6 * n)
  if (window + hmax > n) stop("Not enough data for chosen window and horizon.")

  origins <- window:(n - hmax)
  n_origins <- length(origins)
  if (n_origins <= 0) stop("Not enough data for chosen window and horizon.")

  x_slice <- function(X, idx) {
    if (is.null(dim(X))) return(X[idx])
    X[idx, , drop = FALSE]
  }

  out <- vector("list", n_origins * length(horizons))
  pos <- 0L
  pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = n_origins, style = 3)

  for (k in seq_along(origins)) {
    i <- origins[k]
    train_idx <- (i - window + 1):i

    fit <- tryCatch(
      forecast::Arima(
        y[train_idx],
        order = order,
        seasonal = list(order = seas_order, period = m),
        include.constant = include.constant,
        xreg = x_slice(xreg, train_idx),
        method = method,
        lambda = lambda,
        biasadj = biasadj
      ),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(fit)) { setTxtProgressBar(pb, k); next }

    fc <- tryCatch(
      forecast::forecast(fit, h = hmax, xreg = x_slice(xreg, (i+1):(i+hmax))),
      error = function(e) NULL
    )
    if (is.null(fc)) { setTxtProgressBar(pb, k); next }

    for (h in horizons) {
      pos <- pos + 1L
      err <- as.numeric(y[i + h] - fc$mean[h])
      out[[pos]] <- data.frame(
        Method = "Sliding",
        OriginIndex = i,   # ✅ end-of-training-window index (time index)
        Horizon = h,
        Error = err,
        SqError = err^2
      )
    }

    setTxtProgressBar(pb, k)
  }
  close(pb)

  dplyr::bind_rows(out[1:pos])
}
cv_slide_v2 <- cv_sliding_arimax_v2(
  y = y_full_ts,
  xreg = xreg_v2_train,
  m = m,
  order = chosen_spec$order,
  seas_order = chosen_spec$seasonal,
  horizons = c(4, 8, 12),
  window = floor(0.6 * length(y_full_ts)),
  include.constant = TRUE,
  method = "CSS",
  lambda = lambda_bc,
  
  biasadj = TRUE
)

cv_slide_v2 %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Horizon) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    RMSE = sqrt(mean(SqError, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  print()
```

```{r}

  # Combine expanding + sliding (make sure Method exists and differs)
cv_exp_v2$Method   <- "Expanding"
cv_slide_v2$Method <- "Sliding"
cv_arimax_v2_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(cv_exp_v2, cv_slide_v2)

rolling_rmse_stability_arimax_v2 <- function(cv_df, k = 26) {
  cv_df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(Method, Horizon) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(OriginIndex, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      RollingRMSE = zoo::rollapply(
        SqError,
        width = k,
        FUN = function(z) sqrt(mean(z, na.rm = TRUE)),
        fill = NA,
        align = "right"
      )
    ) %>%
    ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(
      x = OriginIndex, y = RollingRMSE, color = Method, group = Method
    )) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, na.rm = TRUE) +
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ Horizon, scales = "free_y") +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = paste0("ARIMAX v2 Rolling RMSE Stability (window = ", k, " origins)"),
      x = "Origin index (end of training window)",
      y = "Rolling RMSE"
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal()
}

# Plot
rolling_rmse_stability_arimax_v2(cv_arimax_v2_all, k = 26)
```

```{r}
cum_rmse_convergence_arimax_v2 <- function(cv_df) {

  cv_df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      OriginIndex = as.integer(OriginIndex),
      SqError = as.numeric(SqError),
      SqError_ok = dplyr::if_else(is.finite(SqError), SqError, NA_real_)
    ) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(Method, Horizon) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(OriginIndex, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      n_eff = cumsum(!is.na(SqError_ok)),
      sse   = cumsum(dplyr::coalesce(SqError_ok, 0)),
      CumRMSE = sqrt(sse / n_eff)
    ) %>%
    ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(x = OriginIndex, y = CumRMSE, color = Method)) +
    ggplot2::geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, na.rm = TRUE) +
    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~ Horizon, scales = "free_y") +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = "Cumulative RMSE Convergence (ARIMAX v2: Expanding vs Sliding)",
      x = "Origin index (end of training window)",
      y = "Cumulative RMSE"
    ) +
    ggplot2::theme_minimal()
}

cum_rmse_convergence_arimax_v2(cv_arimax_v2_all)
```

```{r}

roll_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(cv_exp_v2, cv_slide_v2) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Method = factor(Method, levels = c("Expanding","Sliding")),
    Horizon = as.integer(Horizon)
  )

# Option A (recommended): CV RMSE = sqrt(mean SqError) across all origins
summary_by_h <- roll_all %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Method, Horizon) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    RMSE = sqrt(mean(SqError, na.rm = TRUE)),
    N = sum(!is.na(SqError)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
ggplot(summary_by_h, aes(x = Method, y = RMSE, fill = Method)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ Horizon, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "ARIMAX v2: RMSE Comparison (Expanding vs Sliding)",
    x = "CV Method",
    y = "RMSE",
    fill = "Method"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Expanding" = "#F8766D",
      "Sliding"   = "#00BFC4"
    )
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
cv_arimax_v2_all %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Error = sqrt(SqError)) %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(x = factor(Horizon), y = abs(Error), fill = Method)) +
  ggplot2::geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.3) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Absolute Forecast Error Distribution by Horizon (ARIMAX v2)",
    x = "Horizon (weeks)",
    y = "|Forecast error|",
    fill = "CV Method"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal()

```

Rolling RMSE Stability

The rolling RMSE plot evaluates the short-term stability of forecast accuracy across time. Both expanding and sliding window approaches show similar temporal patterns, with errors increasing sharply during certain periods, indicating times when influenza dynamics became harder to predict. However, the sliding window consistently produces slightly lower rolling RMSE than the expanding window, suggesting that models trained on more recent data adapt better to evolving patterns in influenza activity.

Cumulative RMSE Convergence

The cumulative RMSE curves illustrate how cross-validation error stabilizes as more rolling origins are included. Early in the evaluation period the RMSE fluctuates substantially due to limited observations, but it gradually converges as additional forecasts are incorporated. Across all horizons, the sliding window maintains a lower cumulative RMSE than the expanding window, indicating that using a fixed-length recent training window yields more reliable and stable forecast performance.

RMSE Comparison (Expanding vs Sliding)

The RMSE comparison summarizes cross-validation performance across forecast horizons. For all horizons (4, 8, and 12 weeks), the sliding window approach achieves lower RMSE than the expanding window. Forecast accuracy decreases as the horizon increases, which is expected due to increasing uncertainty in longer-term predictions. Overall, the results indicate that restricting training to more recent observations improves ARIMAX v2 forecast accuracy.

Absolute Forecast Error Distribution

The boxplots show the distribution of absolute forecast errors across horizons for both cross-validation strategies. Errors increase and become more dispersed as the forecast horizon grows, reflecting greater uncertainty in longer-term predictions. The sliding window generally exhibits slightly lower median errors and tighter interquartile ranges compared to the expanding window, reinforcing the conclusion that models trained on recent data produce more accurate and consistent forecasts.
